content.text: Voice Input to Claude Text Pipeline

A voice pipeline converts a user's spoken words into a Claude-powered response, optionally reading the answer back aloud. In Expo, the pipeline runs across three APIs: expo-av for audio capture, a speech-to-text service (OpenAI Whisper or on-device recognition) for transcription, and the Anthropic SDK for reasoning. The resulting text can be shown in a chat UI and optionally voiced with expo-speech. This document covers each stage end-to-end with production-ready code.

attrs.alt: Voice pipeline: Capture → Transcribe → Reason (Claude) → Speak → Present
attrs.title: Four-stage voice-to-Claude pipeline with recording state machine

Whisper is the most accurate off-device transcription option. Send the audio file from expo-av directly to the Whisper API using the
web API — no extra library needed:
For apps that need offline support or want to avoid sending audio to a third party,
wraps the platform's built-in recognizer (Apple's Speech framework on iOS, Android's SpeechRecognizer). It doesn't require a recorded file — it streams recognition results in real time:
Stage 3: Sending the Transcript to Claude
Once transcription completes, send the text to Claude exactly as you would any chat message. Use

Tell Claude the reply will be read aloud. This suppresses markdown (bullets, code fences, bold asterisks) that sounds unnatural when spoken, and encourages Claude to produce shorter, more conversational answers.
expo-speech is a thin wrapper around the platform's on-device TTS engine — iOS's AVSpeechSynthesizer and Android's TextToSpeech. Install it and call
The component below combines all four stages. It tracks a

exposes your key in the JS bundle. For production, proxy the Whisper call through your backend — call your endpoint instead of
If the user presses the mic while Claude's response is still being read aloud, call
. Overlapping TTS and recording causes distortion and failed transcriptions.
Whisper returns an empty string for audio under ~0.5 seconds. Guard against it before sending to Claude — a blank user message returns an error from the Anthropic API.
The pipeline has four stages: record (expo-av), transcribe (Whisper or on-device), reason (Anthropic SDK streaming), and optionally speak (expo-speech).
enum to keep the UI in sync — disable the mic button while transcribing or thinking.
Whisper gives high accuracy across many languages at $0.006/min; expo-speech-recognition is free, offline, and private but English-centric.
Tell Claude in the system prompt that replies will be read aloud — it will avoid markdown formatting and keep answers short.
Reset iOS audio mode after every recording, guard against empty transcripts, and proxy API keys through your backend for production apps.
